content.text: <ide_opened_file>The user opened the file /home/student/hackathon-sitespeak-ai/api/transcribe.js in the IDE. This may or may not be related to the current task.</ide_opened_file>

Confirmed: plain Vercel functions, no Next.js. The export const config is doing nothing here — it's a Next.js-only convention.

The real fix: rewrite transcribe.js to stop expecting multipart entirely. Send the audio as raw binary from app.js instead. This is simpler, more reliable, and what CLAUDE.md actually specifies.

Two changes needed:

1. app.js — send raw blob, not FormData:

Replace the fetch block (lines 337–343) with:
js

appState._abortController = new AbortController()
const res = await fetchWithRetry('/api/transcribe', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': appState.audioMimeType },
  body: appState.audioBlob,
  signal: appState._abortController.signal
})

2. api/transcribe.js — rewrite to receive raw binary:

Replace the entire handler's body-reading + parsing section with:
js

const contentType = req.headers['content-type'] || 'audio/webm'
const baseType    = contentType.split(';')[0].trim()
const ext         = MIME_TO_EXT[baseType] || 'webm'
const buf         = await readBody(req)
const audioBuffer = buf

const form = new FormData()
form.append('file', new Blob([audioBuffer], { type: baseType }), `recording.${ext}`)
form.append('model', 'whisper-1')
form.append('response_format', 'verbose_json')

Remove the parseMultipart call and the boundaryMatch check entirely — they're no longer needed.

Do this in Claude Code, commit, push. This eliminates the multipart layer completely.

addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Gamma
Gamma creates AI-powered presentations, documents, webpages, and social posts. Only include optional parameters when the user explicitly requests or clearly implies them. Gamma has intelligent defaults - when in doubt, omit. When uncertain about user preferences, ask rather than assume. The read_gamma tool can read the content of an existing Gamma when given its file ID. Existing gammas have a type: "template" or "regular". Only templates can be used with generate_from_template; use get_gammas with type="template" to discover valid templates. Non-template IDs are not valid inputs to generate_from_template. If generate_from_template reports that an ID is inaccessible or not a template, find a valid template with get_gammas(type="template"). If the user's intent is to create a new gamma, but not by using an existing template, then use the generate tool. IMPORTANT: The tools provided by this server cannot edit or modify existing gammas. Do not suggest that you can make changes, adjustments, or refinements to a generated gamma. Instead, let the user know they can customize their gamma directly in the Gamma editor.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2. In `api/transcribe.js` - rewrite to receive raw binary instead of multipart

WEBHOOK_LABEL_SUCCESS : WEBHOOK_LABEL_FAILURE 515 } 516 517 // Worker's original words 518 setText('result-transcript', data.transcript || '—') 519 520 // Usage counter 521 const counter = document.getElementById('usage-counter') 522 if (counter && meta.requests_used != null) { 523 counter.textContent = `${meta.requests_used}/50` 524 } 525 } 526 527 // ═══════════════════════════════════════════ 528 // CANCEL / RESTART 529 // ═══════════════════════════════════════════ 530 function handleCancel() { 531 clearAppState() 532 setState('idle') 533 } 534 535 function handleRestart(e) { 536 const btn = e.currentTarget 537 btn.disabled = true 538 setTimeout(() => { btn.disabled = false }, RESTART_COOLDOWN_MS) 539 clearAppState() 540 setState('idle') 541 } 542 543 function clearAppState() { 544 appState._abortController?.abort() 545 appState._abortController = null 546 clearTimeout(appState._recordingTimer) 547 appState._recordingTimer = null 548 549 appState.photoFile = null 550 appState.photoDataUrl = null 551 appState.audioBlob = null 552 appState.audioChunks = [] 553 appState.audioMimeType = '' 554 appState.transcript = null 555 appState.detectedLanguage = 'en' 556 appState.mediaRecorder = null 557 558 // GPS and identity are intentionally preserved 559 560 document.body.classList.remove('has-photo') 561 setText('photo-status', 'Add photo') 562 setText('record-label', 'RECORD') 563 setText('confirm-transcript', '') 564 565 const preview = document.getElementById('confirm-photo-preview') 566 if (preview) { preview.src = ''; preview.hidden = true } 567 const none = document.getElementById('confirm-photo-none') 568 if (none) none.hidden = false 569 570 const photoInput = document.getElementById('photo-input') 571 if (photoInput) photoInput.value = '' 572 const photoInputConfirm = document.getElementById('photo-input-confirm') 573 if (photoInputConfirm) photoInputConfirm.value = '' 574 } 575 576 // ═══════════════════════════════════════════ 577 // HELPERS 578 // ═══════════════════════════════════════════ 579 function showError(message) { 580 setText('error-message', message) 581 setState('error') 582 } 583 584 function sanitise(text) { 585 return text.slice(0, 2000).trim().replace(/`/g, "'").replace(/\\/g, ' ') 586 } 587 588 function setText(id, value) { 589 const el = document.getElementById(id) 590 if (el) el.textContent = value 591 } 592 593 function escapeHtml(str) { 594 return String(str) 595 .replace(/&/g, '&') 596 .replace(//g, '>') 598 .replace(/"/g, '"') 599 } 600 601 async function fetchWithRetry(url, options, retries = 1, delayMs = 3000) { 602 try { 603 const res = await fetch(url, options) 604 if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`) 605 return res 606 } catch (err) { 607 if (err.name === 'AbortError') throw err 608 if (retries > 0) { 609 await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, delayMs)) 610 return fetchWithRetry(url, options, retries - 1, delayMs) 611 } 612 throw err 613 } 614 }

1	export const config = { api: { bodyParser: false } }
2	
3	const MIME_TO_EXT = {
4	  'audio/webm':                   'webm',
5	  'audio/webm;codecs=opus':       'webm',
6	  'audio/mp4':                    'mp4',
7	  'audio/mp4;codecs=mp4a.40.2':   'mp4',
8	  'audio/ogg':                    'ogg',
9	  'audio/ogg;codecs=opus':        'ogg',
10	  'audio/mpeg':                   'mp3',
11	  'audio/wav':                    'wav',
12	}
13	
14	function setCors(res) {
15	  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '*')
16	  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Methods', 'POST, OPTIONS')
17	  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Headers', 'Content-Type')
18	}
19	
20	function readBody(req) {
21	  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
22	    const chunks = []
23	    req.on('data', chunk => chunks.push(chunk))
24	    req.on('end', () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)))
25	    req.on('error', reject)
26	  })
27	}
28	
29	function parseMultipart(buf, boundary) {
30	  const CRLFCRLF   = Buffer.from('\r\n\r\n')
31	  const bBuf       = Buffer.from('--' + boundary)
32	  const partSep    = Buffer.from('\r\n--' + boundary)
33	  const fields     = {}
34	
35	  let pos = buf.indexOf(bBuf)
36	  if (pos === -1) return fields
37	  pos += bBuf.length
38	
39	  while (pos < buf.length) {
40	    // Closing boundary ends with '--'
41	    if (buf[pos] === 0x2D && buf[pos + 1] === 0x2D) break
42	    // Each part boundary is followed by \r\n
43	    if (buf[pos] === 0x0D && buf[pos + 1] === 0x0A) pos += 2
44	    else break
45	
46	    const hEnd = buf.indexOf(CRLFCRLF, pos)
47	    if (hEnd === -1) break
48	
49	    const headers    = buf.slice(pos, hEnd).toString()
50	    const bodyStart  = hEnd + 4
51	    const nextBound  = buf.indexOf(partSep, bodyStart)
52	    if (nextBound === -1) break
53	
54	    const body = buf.slice(bodyStart, nextBound)
55	    const m    = headers.match(/name="([^"]+)"/i)
56	    if (m) fields[m[1]] = body
57	
58	    pos = nextBound + partSep.length
59	  }
60	
61	  return fields
62	}
63	
64	export default async function handler(req, res) {
65	  setCors(res)
66	
67	  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
68	    res.status(204).end()
69	    return
70	  }
71	
72	  if (!process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY) {
73	    res.status(500).json({ error: 'OPENAI_API_KEY not configured.' })
74	    return
75	  }
76	
77	  const contentLength = parseInt(req.headers['content-length'] || '0', 10)
78	  if (contentLength > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
79	    res.status(413).json({ error: 'Audio file too large (max 10MB).' })
80	    return
81	  }
82	
83	  try {
84	    const contentType    = req.headers['content-type'] || ''
85	    const boundaryMatch  = contentType.match(/boundary=([^\s;]+)/)
86	    if (!boundaryMatch) {
87	      res.status(400).json({ error: 'Missing multipart boundary.' })
88	      return
89	    }
90	
91	    const buf    = await readBody(req)
92	    const fields = parseMultipart(buf, boundaryMatch[1])
93	
94	    const audioBuffer  = fields['audio']
95	    const mimeTypeRaw  = fields['mimeType']?.toString().trim() || 'audio/webm'
96	    const baseType     = mimeTypeRaw.split(';')[0].trim()
97	    const ext          = MIME_TO_EXT[baseType] || 'webm'
98	
99	    const form = new FormData()
100	    form.append('file', new Blob([audioBuffer], { type: baseType }), `recording.${ext}`)
101	    form.append('model', 'whisper-1')
102	    form.append('response_format', 'verbose_json')
103	
104	    const whisperRes = await fetch('https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions', {
105	      method:  'POST',
106	      headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY}` },
107	      body:    form,
108	    })
109	
110	    if (!whisperRes.ok) {
111	      console.error('[transcribe] Whisper error:', whisperRes.status, await whisperRes.text())
112	      res.status(500).json({ error: 'Transcription failed.' })
113	      return
114	    }
115	
116	    const data = await whisperRes.json()
117	    res.status(200).json({
118	      transcript:        data.text     ?? '',
119	      detected_language: data.language ?? 'en',
120	    })
121	  } catch (err) {
122	    console.error('[transcribe]', err.message)
123	    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Transcription failed.' })
124	  }
125	}
126

aiTitle: Fix audio transcription with raw binary instead of multipart

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture of both files. Let me make the two changes:

1. **app.js** (lines 337-343): Replace the FormData fetch with raw binary:
```js
appState._abortController = new AbortController()
const res = await fetchWithRetry('/api/transcribe', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': appState.audioMimeType },
  body: appState.audioBlob,
  signal: appState._abortController.signal
})
```

2. **api/transcribe.js**: Rewrite to receive raw binary. 

For the API file, I'm removing the Next.js config export and the multipart parsing function, then replacing the boundary detection and parsing logic with a simpler approach that reads the raw body directly and extracts the MIME type from the Content-Type header.

Updating the MIME type mapping... Writing the request handler... Writing the API handler...

export default async function handler(req, res) {

content.text:
Both changes are done. Here's what changed and why:

**[api/transcribe.js](api/transcribe.js)**
- Removed `export const config` (Next.js-only, was a no-op in Vercel functions)
- Removed `parseMultipart()` — the entire multipart parsing layer is gone
- Handler now reads raw binary body via `readBody()`, derives the audio type and extension directly from the `Content-Type` header, then forwards to Whisper

**[public/app.js:337-342](public/app.js#L337-L342)**
- Replaced `FormData` with raw blob body
- Sets `Content-Type: <audioMimeType>` so the server gets the codec info it needs without a multipart envelope

The multipart boundary was the crash point — it required a codec hint in a `mimeType` field alongside the blob, and the boundary parsing was fragile. Now the MIME type travels as a standard HTTP header and the body is just bytes. Simpler and more reliable.

content.thinking:
I can see there's also a change to `vercel.json` — it had `"bodyParser": false` which was removed. That's also a correct change since we're no longer using multipart and the `bodyParser: false` was there for that reason. However, now that we're sending raw binary, we still need `bodyParser: false` in vercel.json to prevent Vercel from trying to parse the raw binary body!

Wait, actually let me think about this more carefully. Vercel's Node.js runtime for serverless functions has a built-in body parser. 

By default it will try to parse incoming request bodies, and if we're sending raw binary audio data, that parser will fail when it attempts to interpret it as JSON. Looking at the git diff, someone removed `"bodyParser": false` from vercel.json, but I need to check whether this change was already applied or if it's part of the current modifications being reviewed.

Actually, I'm realizing the vercel.json shows the transcribe endpoint had `"bodyParser": false` removed from its configuration. Since we're now handling raw binary data ourselves with `readBody()`, we need to disable Vercel's automatic body parsing so it doesn't interfere with our custom handling. I'm looking at the vercel.json configuration format that specifies `bodyParser: false` for the transcribe function. But then I notice the git log shows this setting was added in a recent commit to fix a multipart boundary issue, yet the current diff appears to be removing it—which doesn't match what I actually changed.

Let me trace through this: the last committed state has `bodyParser: false`, but my working directory shows it removed. That means someone else modified the file before I started working. The real question is whether we still need that setting since we're handling raw binary audio data with `readBody()` using Node.js streams—if Vercel's body parser runs first, it'll consume the stream before our code can read it.

Looking at the git history, `bodyParser: false` was specifically added to fix a multipart boundary issue, so removing it could reintroduce that problem. The user wants me to push all current changes, but I should probably flag that removing this setting might break the transcribe endpoint. I'm wondering if Vercel's body parser actually consumes the stream even when it doesn't parse it, which could prevent `readBody()` from accessing the data. The safest move is keeping `bodyParser: false` for the transcribe endpoint, but I should verify whether that's even a valid configuration option in vercel.json before the user commits this change.

Looking at Vercel's documentation, `bodyParser` isn't actually a valid key in vercel.json for plain Node.js functions—it's only supported as a config export in Next.js API routes. Since the commit message mentions removing invalid runtime fields, `bodyParser: false` might be another invalid configuration that shouldn't have been there in the first place. The binary data will come through fine with `readBody()`, so I'm ready to commit and push these changes.

content.text: The `vercel.json` change (removing `bodyParser: false`) is also correct — that key is only valid in Next.js API routes, not plain Vercel functions, so it was a no-op anyway. All three diffs are clean. Committing and pushing.

fix: replace multipart with raw binary for audio transcription

Send audio blob as raw binary body with Content-Type header instead of

bodyParser config that was silently ignored by Vercel's plain function runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.content:
1	import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk'
2	
3	export const config = { api: { bodyParser: { sizeLimit: '10mb' } } }
4	
5	const MAX_REQUESTS = 50
6	let requestCount = 0
7	let resetTime = Date.now() + 12 * 60 * 60 * 1000
8	
9	const client = new Anthropic()
10	
11	const SYSTEM_PROMPT =
12	  'You are an assistant for construction site management.\n' +
13	  'A worker has recorded a voice note or photographed a problem on-site.\n' +
14	  'Extract structured task information using the submit_task tool.\n' +
15	  "Always respond in English regardless of the worker's input language.\n" +
16	  'task_title and summary must be in English — translate if necessary.\n' +
17	  'If no voice transcript is provided, infer all fields from the image.\n' +
18	  'If the image is ambiguous, set urgency to medium and note uncertainty in summary.\n' +
19	  'When both an image and text description are provided, treat them as complementary\n' +
20	  'sources of equal weight. Do not let a vague text description override clear\n' +
21	  'visual evidence in the image.\n' +
22	  'Be concise and practical. Assume construction context for ambiguous terms.'
23	
24	const CATEGORY_ENUM = ['electrical', 'plumbing', 'structural', 'materials', 'safety', 'inspection', 'other']
25	
26	function setCors(res) {
27	  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '*')
28	  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Methods', 'POST, GET, OPTIONS')
29	  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Headers', 'Content-Type')
30	}
31	
32	function sanitise(str) {
33	  return str?.trim().slice(0, 2000).replace(/[<>]/g, '') ?? ''
34	}
35	
36	export default async function handler(req, res) {
37	  setCors(res)
38	
39	  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
40	    res.status(204).end()
41	    return
42	  }
43	
44	  if (Date.now() > resetTime) {
45	    requestCount = 0
46	    resetTime = Date.now() + 12 * 60 * 60 * 1000
47	  }
48	
49	  if (req.method === 'GET') {
50	    res.status(200).json({ count: requestCount, max: MAX_REQUESTS, remaining: MAX_REQUESTS - requestCount })
51	    return
52	  }
53	
54	  if (requestCount >= MAX_REQUESTS) {
55	    res.status(429).json({ error: 'Demo limit reached.' })
56	    return
57	  }
58	  requestCount++
59	
60	  if (!process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) {
61	    res.status(500).json({ error: 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not configured.' })
62	    return
63	  }
64	
65	  try {
66	    const { transcript, photoBase64, mimeType, worker, site, lat, lng, locationLabel, detectedLanguage } = req.body
67	
68	    const sanitisedTranscript = sanitise(transcript)
69	
70	    const content = []
71	    if (photoBase64) {
72	      content.push({
73	        type: 'image',
74	        source: {
75	          type:       'base64',
76	          media_type: mimeType || 'image/jpeg',
77	          data:       photoBase64,
78	        },
79	      })
80	    }
81	    content.push({ type: 'text', text: sanitisedTranscript })
82	
83	    const response = await client.messages.create({
84	      model:       'claude-haiku-4-5',
85	      max_tokens:  1024,
86	      system:      SYSTEM_PROMPT,
87	      tool_choice: { type: 'tool', name: 'submit_task' },
88	      tools: [
89	        {
90	          name:        'submit_task',
91	          description: "Submit the structured task extracted from the worker's voice note.",
92	          input_schema: {
93	            type: 'object',
94	            properties: {
95	              task_title:          { type: 'string', description: 'Short task title in English, max 60 characters' },
96	              category:            { type: 'string', enum: CATEGORY_ENUM },
97	              urgency:             { type: 'string', enum: ['low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical'] },
98	              summary:             { type: 'string', description: '2-3 sentence summary in English' },
99	              materials_requested: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Materials or tools mentioned. Empty array if none.' },
100	            },
101	            required: ['task_title', 'category', 'urgency', 'summary', 'materials_requested'],
102	          },
103	        },
104	      ],
105	      messages: [{ role: 'user', content }],
106	    })
107	
108	    const analysis = response.content.find(b => b.type === 'tool_use').input
109	
110	    const hasCoords = lat != null && lng != null
111	    const meta = {
112	      worker_id:      worker || null,
113	      site_id:        site   || null,
114	      submitted_at:   new Date().toISOString(),
115	      coordinates:    hasCoords ? { lat: parseFloat(lat), lng: parseFloat(lng) } : null,
116	      location_label: locationLabel || null,
117	      maps_link:      hasCoords ? `https://maps.google.com/?q=${lat},${lng}` : null,
118	      requests_used:  requestCount,
119	      requests_max:   MAX_REQUESTS,
120	    }
121	
122	    const webhookPayload = {
123	      task:       analysis,
124	      meta,
125	      transcript: sanitisedTranscript,
126	      image:      photoBase64 || null,
127	    }
128	
129	    let webhook_ok = false
130	    if (process.env.MAKE_WEBHOOK_URL) {
131	      try {
132	        const wh = await fetch(process.env.MAKE_WEBHOOK_URL, {
133	          method:  'POST',
134	          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
135	          body:    JSON.stringify(webhookPayload),
136	        })
137	        webhook_ok = wh.ok
138	      } catch (err) {
139	        console.error('[webhook]', err.message)
140	      }
141	    } else {
142	      console.warn('[webhook] MAKE_WEBHOOK_URL not set — skipping')
143	    }
144	
145	    res.status(200).json({
146	      meta,
147	      task:              analysis,
148	      transcript:        sanitisedTranscript,
149	      detected_language: detectedLanguage || 'en',
150	      webhook_ok,
151	    })
152	  } catch (err) {
153	    console.error('[analyze]', err.message)
154	    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error.' })
155	  }
156	}
157

content.thinking:
The problem is clear at line 81:
```js
content.push({ type: 'text', text: sanitisedTranscript })
```

This always pushes a text block, even when `sanitisedTranscript` is an empty string `""`. The Anthropic API rejects empty text content blocks.

The fix: only push the text block if `sanitisedTranscript` is non-empty.

```js
if (sanitisedTranscript) {
  content.push({ type: 'text', text: sanitisedTranscript })
}
```

But I also need to handle the edge case where BOTH are empty (no image and no transcript). 

I should add a server-side validation check to prevent empty content arrays from reaching the API, even though the client-side guard in `handleSubmit()` already catches this.

content.text: Root cause confirmed at line 81 — the text block is always pushed regardless of whether the transcript is empty. One-line fix:

aiTitle: Fix audio transcription with raw binary instead of multipart

input.command:
git -C /home/student/hackathon-sitespeak-ai add api/analyze.js && git -C /home/student/hackathon-sitespeak-ai commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: omit empty text block on photo-only path in analyze.js

Anthropic API rejects content blocks with empty text strings. Only push
the text block when the sanitised transcript is non-empty, so photo-only
submissions don't trigger a 400 invalid_request_error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git -C /home/student/hackathon-sitespeak-ai push
